Parth Parmar
About Resume Blog

March 4, 2026 - Platform, Serving

Designing ML platforms around real operational constraints

The quality of an ML platform is usually determined less by its conceptual elegance than by how well it fits the constraints of the teams and services depending on it.

Platform work often goes wrong when teams optimize for theoretical flexibility instead of operational truth. A training platform may need strict conventions because the people using it are not platform specialists. An inference stack may need fewer extension points and better observability because paging frequency matters more than abstraction purity.

Constraints like latency budgets, GPU availability, run reproducibility, rollback safety, or artifact lineage are not secondary concerns. They define the system. If the platform ignores them, teams will route around it.

Operational constraints clarify ownership

Strong platforms draw a line between what teams can self-serve and what the platform itself guarantees. That boundary should be shaped by support capacity and failure modes, not by a desire to look maximally general.

Design for the steady state

Most ML infrastructure lives in the boring middle: repeated runs, recurring incidents, changing schemas, and evolving models. Good platform design should make that steady state easier to manage rather than optimizing only for greenfield demos.

Parth Parmar.
